title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: Neuroimaging research produces collections derived maps (statistical maps, anatomical scans, ROI vectors) across subjects, conditions, sessions, sites. Today collections exist ad-hoc directories files metadata scattered across spreadsheets, BIDS sidecars, -memory data frames. standard way : Declare collection contains logical level (independent file format) Query, group, compare members using design metadata Concatenate collections different studies storage backends Hand collection downstream tools (viewers, statistical engines, ML pipelines) machine-readable contract forces every tool re-invent file discovery, metadata joining, format detection.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: neurotabs two things: NFTab — cross-language specification row-oriented neuroimaging datasets, defining observations, design metadata, logical features relate, independent storage backend. neurotabs (R package) — reference implementation: read, write, validate, query, manipulate NFTab datasets dplyr-like grammar. one-sentence version: row observation design metadata one feature values; feature value resolves, declared representation, logical array known semantics.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: UC-1: Import browse. Load directory NIfTI stat maps + CSV design table. Browse subject, condition, group. Flip members. UC-2: Cohort query. Filter group == "patient" & age > 40, compute mean map, compare matched controls. UC-3: Concatenate studies. Two labs ran paradigm different scanners. Concatenate NFTab datasets verifying logical schema compatibility. UC-4: Cross-language handoff. R script prepares NFTab dataset. Rust viewer (Brainflow) reads nftab.yaml manifest, resolves features, renders — R dependency needed. UC-5: Feed statistical engine. Select feature NFTab dataset, resolve rows arrays, hand aligned set fmrigds group-level meta-analysis. UC-6: Publish provenance. Export self-contained NFTab package (manifest + observation table + resources) another researcher can validate reproduce.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: spec seven normative sections:

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
code: Dataset = Manifest + ObservationTable + Optional(ResourceRegistry) Observation = row_id + observation_axes + design columns + feature encoding columns Feature = logical schema + ordered encodings ResolvedFeature(row, feature) = first applicable encoding → logical value

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: Required fields: spec_version, dataset_id, storage_profile, observation_table, row_id, observation_axes, observation_columns, features. Optional: resources, extensions (keys prefixed x-).

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: One row per observation. row_id MUST unique. Tuple observation_axes MUST unique. repeated measurements exist grain, provider MUST add distinguishing axis.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: design column declares: dtype, nullable, semantic_role (optional), levels (optional), unit (optional). Allowed dtypes: string, int32, int64, float32, float64, bool, date, datetime, json. Recommended semantic roles: row_id, subject, session, run, group, condition, contrast, site, covariate.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: feature declares: Logical schema: kind, axes, dtype, shape, axis_domains, space, alignment, unit Encodings: ordered list; first applicable wins per row Logical kinds (descriptive, must contradict axes): array, volume, vector, matrix, surface. Alignment levels: same_grid, same_space, loose, none. Key constraint: shape, present, must match len(axes). Every resolved value must conform logical schema.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: ref encoding: resolves external resource. Must provide either resource_id (looked registry) backend + locator. Selector syntax backend-defined, must pure data (code execution). columns encoding: resolves scalar observation table columns. Draft 0.1: columns encoding 1D logical features.
code: type: ref binding:   resource_id: <literal or {column: "col_name"}>   backend: <literal or {column: "col_name"}>   locator: <literal or {column: "col_name"}>   selector: <literal or {column: "col_name"}>  # optional, JSON-compatible   checksum: <literal or {column: "col_name"}>  # optional type: columns binding:   columns: ["roi_1", "roi_2", ..., "roi_68"]

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: Two datasets strict-concatenation-compatible iff: - major spec_version - Identical observation_axes (content order) - Identical feature name sets - Identical logical feature schemas (excluding descriptive fields) - Compatible scalar column types (dtype safe numeric promotion) Concatenation defined logical schema, physical encoding. Two datasets using different backends feature compatible.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
code: for each encoding in feature.encodings (in order):     if encoding is applicable to row:         value = materialize(encoding, row, registry)         validate(value, feature.logical)         return value if feature.nullable: return MISSING else: ERROR nonconformant

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: Dataset conformance: - Structurally conformant: manifest valid, tables present, uniqueness holds, schemas consistent. - Fully conformant: structurally conformant every non-missing feature resolves validates. Reader conformance: - Core-conformant: can parse manifest observation table, evaluate encoding applicability, resolve columns encodings, report unsupported ref backends explicitly. - Readers required support every backend.

title: neurotabs — Product Requirements Document
text: Spec uses semantic versioning. Major version bumps indicate breaking changes abstract data model. Minor versions may add new optional fields, new encoding types, new storage profiles. New kind values alignment values may added minor versions. Backend-specific selector conventions non-normative versioned independently.

what: How Do You Encode ROI Names And Atlas Identity?
title: Getting Started with neurotabs
text: columns encodings, physical column names just storage. semantic metadata belongs feature axis. practice means: binding.columns defines storage order ROI vector. nf_axis_info(..., "roi")$id identifies atlas parcellation. nf_axis_info(..., "roi")$labels points label table names ROI. shipped faces-demo uses pattern three-region ROI feature. intended encoding parcel-wise vectors. atlas identity lives logical axis, storage column names, logical ROI feature can stored different physical column bundles still mean thing.
code: roi_schema <- nf_feature_schema(faces_ds, "roi_beta") stopifnot(identical(roi_schema$axes, "roi"))  roi_axis <- nf_axis_info(faces_ds, "roi_beta", "roi") stopifnot(identical(roi_axis$id, "desikan3-demo"))  roi_axis #> <nf_axis_domain> #>   id: desikan3-demo  #>   labels: roi_labels.tsv roi_labels <- nf_axis_labels(faces_ds, "roi_beta", "roi") roi_values <- nf_resolve(faces_ds, 1L, "roi_beta") stopifnot(nrow(roi_labels) == length(roi_values))  data.frame(   atlas = roi_axis$id,   label = roi_labels$label,   value = roi_values,   row.names = NULL ) #>           atlas                label value #> 1 desikan3-demo        left_fusiform  0.31 #> 2 desikan3-demo       right_fusiform  0.44 #> 3 desikan3-demo left_parahippocampal  0.29

code: ds <- nf_from_table(   "participants.csv",          # table with subject, condition, etc.   feature     = "statmap",     # name for the feature   locator_col = "nifti_path",  # column pointing to .nii.gz files   space       = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym",   root        = "/data/study" ) ds <- nf_from_table(   design_table,                      # data.frame with subject, condition, etc.   feature = "bold",   locator = "results/group_stats.nii.gz",  # shared 4D file   space   = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym",   root    = "/data/study" ) # Row 1 -> volume 0, row 2 -> volume 1, etc.

title: Getting Started with neurotabs
text: manifest ties columns, features, axes together: combine manifest observation table:
code: manifest <- nf_manifest(   dataset_id          = "my-study",   row_id              = "row_id",   observation_axes    = c("subject", "condition"),   observation_columns = obs_cols,   features            = list(roi = roi_feature) ) observations <- data.frame(   row_id    = c("r1", "r2", "r3", "r4"),   subject   = c("s01", "s01", "s02", "s02"),   condition = c("faces", "houses", "faces", "houses"),   roi_1     = c(0.5, 0.2, 0.6, 0.3),   roi_2     = c(0.4, 0.1, 0.5, 0.2),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  my_ds <- nftab(manifest, observations) my_ds #> <nftab> my-study  #>   4 observations x 1 features #>   axes: subject, condition  #>   features: roi  #>   subject: s01, s02 #>   condition: faces, houses

title: Getting Started with neurotabs
text: full workflow : Read manifest nf_read(). Check structural full conformance nf_validate(). Filter, arrange, select nf_filter(), nf_arrange(), nf_select(). Resolve features nf_resolve() nf_collect(). Apply row-wise operations nf_apply(). Group summarize nf_group_by() + nf_summarize(). Compare groups nf_compare(). Combine compatible datasets nf_concat(). NIfTI-backed features, set options(neurotabs.compute.workers = 4L) enable parallel file reads multi-core systems.

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: NFTab standardizes row-oriented neuroimaging dataset three parts: manifest defines logical contract observation table stores one row per observation optional resource registry externally stored feature data examples shipped neurotabs show simple mixed cases. output corresponds directly abstract data model spec: rows observations, features resolve per row, manifest defines resolved values mean independent physical storage.
code: roi_path <- system.file("examples/roi-only/nftab.yaml", package = "neurotabs") faces_path <- system.file("examples/faces-demo/nftab.yaml", package = "neurotabs") stopifnot(nzchar(roi_path), nzchar(faces_path))  roi_raw <- yaml::read_yaml(roi_path) faces_raw <- yaml::read_yaml(faces_path)  roi_ds <- nf_read(roi_path) faces_ds <- nf_read(faces_path) list(   dataset_id = roi_raw$dataset_id,   storage_profile = roi_raw$storage_profile,   observation_axes = roi_raw$observation_axes,   features = names(roi_raw$features) ) #> $dataset_id #> [1] "roi-only" #>  #> $storage_profile #> [1] "table-package" #>  #> $observation_axes #> [1] "subject"   "condition" #>  #> $features #> [1] "roi_beta"

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: top level, manifest declares dataset identity, storage profile, row identity, observation axes, scalar observation columns, feature set. observation table location part manifest . table-package storage profile, table CSV, TSV, Parquet file travels manifest.
code: roi_raw[c(   "spec_version",   "dataset_id",   "storage_profile",   "row_id",   "observation_axes" )] #> $spec_version #> [1] "0.1.0" #>  #> $dataset_id #> [1] "roi-only" #>  #> $storage_profile #> [1] "table-package" #>  #> $row_id #> [1] "row_id" #>  #> $observation_axes #> [1] "subject"   "condition" roi_raw$observation_table #> $path #> [1] "observations.csv" #>  #> $format #> [1] "csv"

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: observation table row-oriented part dataset. row one observation, scalar design metadata subject, group, condition, age live directly table columns. observation column declared scalar schema. spec uses schema define dtype, nullability, semantic role, levels, optional units. nf_read() loads dataset, coerces table columns declared types rejects incompatible values.
code: utils::head(   roi_ds$observations[c("row_id", "subject", "group", "condition", "age")],   4 ) #>   row_id subject   group condition  age #> 1    r01  sub-01 control     faces 25.3 #> 2    r02  sub-01 control    houses 25.3 #> 3    r03  sub-02 control     faces 31.7 #> 4    r04  sub-02 control    houses 31.7 roi_raw$observation_columns$age #> $dtype #> [1] "float64" #>  #> $nullable #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $semantic_role #> [1] "covariate" #>  #> $unit #> [1] "years"

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: feature just storage column file path. NFTab, feature logical schema defines kind value produced feature resolved one row. ROI example, roi_beta vector one semantic axis called roi, element dtype float32, logical shape 5. Resolving one row produces logical value. separation logical schema physical storage center spec.
code: roi_schema <- nf_feature_schema(roi_ds, "roi_beta") roi_schema #> <nf_logical_schema> vector [5] float32  #>   axes: roi roi_value <- nf_resolve(roi_ds, 1L, "roi_beta") stopifnot(length(roi_value) == 5L)  roi_value #> [1] 0.31 0.44 0.29 0.18 0.22

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: spec allows feature one encodings. encoding says logical feature value can materialized one row. ROI example uses columns encoding: logical vector stored ordered bundle scalar table columns. mixed faces-demo uses ref encoding volume feature. Instead putting voxel data observation table, row carries resource identifier optional selector. spec distinguishes logical features storage backends. feature means “3D statistical map MNI space”, encoding says find particular row.
code: roi_raw$features$roi_beta$encodings[[1]] #> $type #> [1] "columns" #>  #> $binding #> $binding$columns #> [1] "roi_1" "roi_2" "roi_3" "roi_4" "roi_5" faces_raw$features$statmap$encodings[[1]] #> $type #> [1] "ref" #>  #> $binding #> $binding$resource_id #> $binding$resource_id$column #> [1] "stat_res" #>  #>  #> $binding$selector #> $binding$selector$column #> [1] "stat_sel"

what: How Do Axis Domains Carry Atlas Metadata?
title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: Axis domains spec puts semantic metadata feature axis. parcel-wise vectors, encode atlas identity ROI labels. matches intended reading spec: physical binding.columns order defines storage order roi axis defines positions mean axis domain names atlas supplies label table
code: roi_axis <- nf_axis_info(faces_ds, "roi_beta", "roi") roi_labels <- nf_axis_labels(faces_ds, "roi_beta", "roi") stopifnot(nrow(roi_labels) == 3L)  roi_axis #> <nf_axis_domain> #>   id: desikan3-demo  #>   labels: roi_labels.tsv data.frame(   atlas = roi_axis$id,   label = roi_labels$label,   row.names = NULL ) #>           atlas                label #> 1 desikan3-demo        left_fusiform #> 2 desikan3-demo       right_fusiform #> 3 desikan3-demo left_parahippocampal

what: How Do Sparse Masked Volumes Fit?
title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: datasets store dense 3D volume row. Instead, store values inside fixed brain mask. case logical feature better modeled vector voxel axis, together extension maps vector positions back grid coordinates. extension says feature still stored vector, manifest also supplies external index map telling readers (x, y, z) voxel vector position refers . common case masked group maps sparse voxelwise features.
code: masked_manifest <- nf_manifest(   dataset_id = "masked-demo",   row_id = "row_id",   observation_axes = c("subject", "condition"),   observation_columns = list(     row_id = nf_col_schema("string", nullable = FALSE),     subject = nf_col_schema("string", nullable = FALSE),     condition = nf_col_schema("string", nullable = FALSE),     v1 = nf_col_schema("float32"),     v2 = nf_col_schema("float32"),     v3 = nf_col_schema("float32")   ),   features = list(     statvec = nf_feature(       logical = nf_logical_schema(         "vector",         "voxel",         "float32",         shape = 3L,         space = "MNI152NLin2009cAsym",         alignment = "same_grid"       ),       encodings = list(nf_columns_encoding(c("v1", "v2", "v3")))     )   ),   extensions = list(     "x-masked-volume" = list(       features = list(         statvec = list(           grid_axes = c("x", "y", "z"),           grid_shape = c(91L, 109L, 91L),           grid_index_base = 0L,           index_map = list(             path = "voxel_index.tsv",             format = "tsv"           )         )       )     )   ) )  masked_ds <- nftab(   masked_manifest,   observations = data.frame(     row_id = c("r1", "r2"),     subject = c("s01", "s02"),     condition = c("faces", "houses"),     v1 = c(0.1, 0.4),     v2 = c(0.2, 0.5),     v3 = c(0.3, 0.6),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) )  nf_extension(masked_ds, "x-masked-volume") #> $features #> $features$statvec #> $features$statvec$grid_axes #> [1] "x" "y" "z" #>  #> $features$statvec$grid_shape #> [1]  91 109  91 #>  #> $features$statvec$grid_index_base #> [1] 0 #>  #> $features$statvec$index_map #> $features$statvec$index_map$path #> [1] "voxel_index.tsv" #>  #> $features$statvec$index_map$format #> [1] "tsv"

what: What Is The Resource Registry For?
title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: ref encoding can point directly backend locator, many datasets use resource registry rows can refer shared resources resource_id. spec requires registry rows supply storage metadata needed materialize referenced resource, backend name, locator, optionally checksum.
code: faces_raw$resources #> $path #> [1] "resources.csv" #>  #> $format #> [1] "csv" faces_ds$resources #>   resource_id backend                 locator #> 1     group4d   nifti maps/group_stats.nii.gz

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: spec distinguishes structural full conformance. Structural conformance asks whether manifest, table, declared schemas form well-typed NFTab dataset. Full conformance goes attempts resolve every non-missing feature value. ROI-dataset, levels pass feature values live table columns require external backend. datasets ref encodings, full conformance additionally depends backend support resource integrity checks checksum validation.
code: structural <- nf_validate(roi_ds, level = "structural") full <- nf_validate(roi_ds, level = "full") stopifnot(structural$valid, full$valid)  c(structural = structural$valid, full = full$valid) #> structural       full  #>       TRUE       TRUE

title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: spec also defines two datasets compatible enough concatenate. decision depends matching observation axes, compatible scalar columns, identical logical feature schemas. words, NFTab concatenation schema-driven. just row-binding two tables hoping feature values still mean thing.
code: compat <- nf_compatible(roi_ds, roi_ds) stopifnot(compat$compatible)  compat #> $compatible #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $reasons #> character(0)

what: How Should You Read The Full Spec?
title: Understanding the NFTab Specification
text: important sections understand first : Abstract data model Observation table scalar column schema Feature schema logical schema Encodings resource registry Resolution, conformance, compatibility keep five layers mind, rest specification reads precise contract rather collection disconnected fields.
